label: Search, sampling & decoding procedures

node.label: Search, sampling & decoding procedures
node.definition: Candidate outputs or action trajectories are explored, scored, and selected beyond a single greedy pass.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Search, sampling & decoding procedures is to connect Algorithms & data structures, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Self-consistency & best-of-N inference, Beam search & constrained decoding, Test-time compute scaling.
examples: substrate for Beam search & constrained decoding
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Algorithms & data structures, Game theory & decision theory.

dependencies.rationale: Search, sampling & decoding procedures uses Algorithms & data structures as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Search, sampling & decoding procedures uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Search, sampling & decoding procedures uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
